If you're looking for a loyal, gentle buddy who will stick by your side, Felix is ready to meet you!

name: Peaches
description_html: Peaches is a very loving and energetic dog. She loves nothing more than pets, love and treats. She doesn’t know how to play fetch very well but she will love to run around and chase the toys you throw. Peaches is crate trained and does well with other dogs and children. She is very trainable and is very affectionate.

name: Jonah
description_html:
Meet Jonah! 

Jonah is a total sweetheart with a calm, cuddly nature. He once happily napped right on a volunteer in the Real Life Room—proof of just how affectionate and trusting he is. He knows sit, walks well on leash, and has a lovely, relaxed kennel presence.

With his medium energy, playful side, and love of treats, Jonah is an easy companion who enjoys both fun and downtime. If you’re looking for a gentle snuggle buddy with great manners, Jonah could be your perfect match.

name: Daisy
description_html:
~ Meet Daisy


Daisy arrived with a 30lb dog named Zoe (19500) and try as we did, we could not find a home to take both together. Thankfully they seemed to be independent spirits, so two volunteers each took one dog home and they are now loving their new foster homes with companionship from the people and their resident dogs.


Daisy is the sweetest land seal you could ever meet. She fit right into a single story home with a cattle dog (with a strong personality), and an aloof shepherd mix. She likes being near her people, and her dog friends. Working in the kitchen? She would love to park and observe if that's ok. Something have your dogs in a ker fluffer? Daisy's going to take the high road and steer away, she does not like conflict. She never been known to bark. Her sweet affectionate and gentle nature will make her a wonderful fit in many homes, with a range of human and animal friends of all ages. Her advocates at Classic Canines are getting Daisy a comprehensive exam and she's already working on her "New Year, New You" weight goals.

name: Zoe
description_html:
Hello there! I’m Zoe, an adorable 11-year-old pup with a heart full of love and a spirit that’s still a bit spunky! I came into the shelter after my owner sadly passed away, and now I'm looking for my forever home.

These days, I’m living it up with two other dog friends, and I fit in just perfectly! I absolutely adore people and am always ready for my daily strolls around the neighborhood. You’ll often find me stopping to greet everyone I meet—head scratches and belly rubs are my favorite!

I’ve got a curious side, too! I love observing squirrels and cats as they scurry by, and I still enjoy a good game of chase with a ball.

I may be a senior gal, but I’m young at heart and ready to share my love with my forever family. I’m looking for a home, preferably with another dog sibling, as I truly enjoy the company of my doggie friends! If you're on the hunt for a loving companion who will bring joy and laughter to your life, I might just be the girl for you! Feel free to reach out to my foster mom - she'd be happy to share more about me.

name: Sandy
description_html: Sandy is a dream puppy with the perfect puppy energy, fun and joyful. She's affectionate and playful, and will sit for cheese and pets. Sandy is a sweetheart. Come meet her!

name: Josie Rosie
description_html: Meet Josie Rosie, an active tripod girl who would love to be a part of your family!

name: Remington
description_html:
Remington is a big, sweet, toy-loving guy who knows how to enjoy life.

 

He’s got that perfect balance: happy to head out for a walk, toss a toy around, and stretch his legs — and just as happy to settle in close once he’s had his fun. Give him a few minutes and he’ll lean right into you for pets, cuddles, and as much lap time as you’re willing to offer.

 

He can entertain himself with enrichment, greet people with excited tail wags, and then relax like a pro. Medium energy, affectionate, and easy to love — Remington is the kind of dog who fits right into your day.

 

If you’ve been looking for a big-hearted companion with a playful side and a snuggly streak, Remington is ready to meet you.

name: Little Puff
description_html:
Little Puff is a young, curious sweetheart with a soft heart, a smart brain, and a serious appreciation for snacks and snuggles.


Little Puff may be small in name, but he’s full of personality. He’s a curious kid who wants to check out the world, then curl up close once the exploring is done. He’s treat-motivated (read: VERY trainable) and eager to learn, making him a great candidate for basic training and beyond.


This guy works for treats and is proud of it. Whether it’s learning new skills or practicing good manners, Little Puff is ready to focus if snacks are involved.


After he’s satisfied his curiosity, Little Puff is all about affection. He loves being close to his people and has a natural talent for cozy moments.


Young, loving, and ready to learn, Little Puff is looking for someone who wants a dog that’s equal parts playful, trainable, and cuddly. He’s ready to puff up your life in the best way.


To learn more about Little Puff, reach out at blueplusbusters@gmail.com

name: Gemma
description_html:
Gemma is a super affectionate girl around 2 years old (possibly younger based on her teeth). She’s likely an American Bully or Staffy mix and weighs about 62 lbs.

Gemma’s favorite thing in the world is being close to her people. She’s a total cuddle bug who loves leaning on you, touching you, and sleeping right on top of you like a weighted blanket. She has a goofy, funny personality and is incredibly sweet with people.

She has low-to-medium energy and is happy with a mix of playtime (fetch, tug, chew toys) and lots of naps. She can do well in an apartment as long as she gets daily walks.

Gemma is very strong and currently a big puller on leash, especially when she sees other dogs, so continued leash training will be important. That said, she has shown no aggression on walks and rarely barks. She takes treats gently and is food-motivated, which should make training easier.

At home, Gemma is a dream. She’s fully potty trained with no accidents, sleeps through the night, and is not destructive. She currently lives with two adults and has done great. Her compatibility with other dogs, cats, and kids is still being evaluated; due to her size and excitement level, she’d do best with children 10+. Slow, thoughtful introductions to other dogs are recommended.

If you’re looking for a cuddly, loyal companion with a big heart and a great sense of humor, Gemma might be your perfect match.

See more of her at @keep.austin.furry 💛

name: Mama Blythe
description_html: Mama Blythe had some time in foster care where she was extremely sweet and curious towards people. While Mama Blythe enjoys the company of her human friends, she may prefer to be an only pet in the home. This girl is calm and friendly with people, all that she's missing is a sofa and best friend to snuggle up with.

name: Luke
description_html:
Luke is a sweet, energetic boy who was found abandoned just before Christmas and is now looking for his forever home.


He has zero aggression and absolutely loves to play—tug of war, tennis balls, footballs, and any toy he can get his paws on. Luke is very affectionate and enjoys being around people, dogs, and even cats.


At about 1.5 years old, he’s still a big puppy who will thrive with continued training, structure, and lots of love. He’s a smart boy who learns quickly but, like many young dogs, needs consistency and guidance.


Luke would do great as an only dog or with other dogs his size. He currently lives with three small dogs who are a little intimidated by his enthusiasm, but he just wants to be friends.


If you're looking for a playful, loving companion who will bring a lot of joy and personality into your life, Luke might be the perfect match.

name: Reina
description_html: Reina is an adorable, small, wiggly girl. She is shy at first but warms up quickly into a goofy, cuddly goober. She loves rolling in the grass, getting belly and booty rubs, and snuggling in your lap.

name: Toby
description_html:
Meet Toby, a gentle soul residing at the local shelter. Initially shy, Toby warms up to reveal a

curious and sweet nature. Once comfortable, he unveils a playful side that endears him to all

who spend time with him. Toby's tender approach makes him a perfect companion for quiet

evenings and leisurely walks, where he can explore and learn at his own pace. If you're looking

for a loyal friend who will slowly but surely steal your heart, Toby is your ideal match. His gentle

spirit is just waiting for a place to call home.


Toby is:

  • Housetrained
  • Cuddly with friends
  • Playful and loves a game of fetch, though he may not bring it back
  • Great on leash
  • Extremely smart - will blow through any treat puzzle we give him!
  • An old soul type with some pep in his step

name: Morgan
description_html:
Morgan is shy and nervous, and he’s looking for a quiet household where he can keep working on his people skills. He and his litter mates were rescued and brought to AAS after they lost their mom.

Morgan is a member of our Cactus Cats program, and he has progressed well in the shelter. He’s really good at “nose boops” for Churu, and this is an easy confidence training that can be continued in the home.

Morgan needs a patient adopter, and he would benefit from having a cat buddy to hang out with.

I am a DHC, and I have my own webpage!

The adoption fee is waived for Morgan and ALL Cactus Cats/Desperate Housecats!

name: Brutus
description_html:
Brutus is a gentle giant. He lounges around most of the day with 3 cats and a bunny. The cats often take over his bed and he allows it. He loves to go on walks and take in all the smells. He is very curious about other dogs but enjoys his space.

A few things to note, he is terrified of thunder, fireworks and any other loud noises. Walks on trash day aren't his favorite and he refuses to go outside if fireworks are being set off... even from quite a distance.

He has so much love to offer and is the perfect mellow couch potato. Crate trained but free roams and is non destructive.

name: Rosco
description_html:
Meet Rosco! 🐾

Rosco is a friendly, easygoing guy who loves getting out to walk and explore. He’s great on leash and enjoys everything from relaxed strolls to more active adventures—he’s even been a favorite jogging buddy with trail volunteers!

He can be a little shy when meeting new people, but it doesn’t take long for his warm, affectionate side to come out. Rosco is playful, enjoys attention (especially butt scratches), and has a funny, charming personality. He also loves to show off his skills like sit and shake—this smart boy is eager to keep learning.

Rosco has lived in a home before and may do best without cats. If you’re looking for a loyal, fun companion for walks, playtime, and laughs, Rosco is ready to meet you.

name: Cheddar
description_html:
Meet Cheddar, a handsome boy who is ready to turn the page on his past. For most of his life he was a stray, navigating the world on his own until the staff at AAS rescued him. After several months, he found his way into a foster home, and he has absolutely blossomed.

While Cheddar is still a shy boy who needs time to adjust, he is becoming the sweetest companion you could ask for. He is a big talker who will happily tell you about his day. He is a soulmate cat who loves to be petted and thrives on being near his person.

Cheddar shares his foster home with a large Goldendoodle and another male cat, and he totally loves his friends. He adores grooming his feline brother and often looks to his canine friend for comfort and protection.

Cheddar is excellent at communicating his needs—if he gets overwhelmed, he knows exactly where to go to find a quiet, private nap spot. He is looking for a very experienced cat owner and a stable, quiet forever home where he can continue to build his confidence and finally be the sweet boy he was always meant to be.

I am a DHC, and I have my own webpage!

The adoption fee is waived for Cheddar and ALL Desperate Housecats!

name: Griselda
description_html: Looking for a happy, easy-going, sweet dog? Griselda "Grizzy" is for you! She'll always greet you with a waggy tail & lots of licks! She loves cuddles, long naps & outside adventures. She's leash trained, as well as potty trained & crate trained. She know basic commands (sit, stay, down, come) & will be eager to impress you, especially when treats are involved. She'd be a great fit for almost any type of household, including a home with kids or an elderly person. In her current foster home, she gets along well with their energetic 50 lb doodle, even though she's 1/3 in size. A warning about Grizzy: she's bound to steal your heart the 1st time you meet!

name: Coco Melon
description_html:
Do you have a soft spot for the shy girls who turn into total goofballs once they trust you?


Coco Melon lost her home because someone in the household had allergies, and now this sweet, floppy girl is trying to make sense of shelter life. She can be shy at first and may hang back when meeting new people, but don't be fooled. Once you are a friend, Coco Melon lights up in the best way. 


The version of Coco Melon her friends get to know is bouncy, playful, loving, and full of charm. She loves squeaky toys, enjoys being the center of attention, and already knows commands like sit, down, and paw in Spanish. She is the kind of dog who will surprise you, whether it is her goofy curiosity, her eagerness to connect, or the happy little ways her personality starts to unfold once she feels safe. 


Right now, Coco Melon is still figuring out the shelter environment, but she does not belong here. She belongs in a home where she can exhale, play, be silly, and keep showing off the loving dog she really is. Give her a chance, and you just might feel like you hit the lottery

name: Bandit
description_html:
Bandit arrived with a young lady dog, displaced after a house fire. Despite that big interruption to his life, he's a cheerful guy who loves and bonds sweetly with all of his fans and helpers here. He loves opportunities to be outdoorsy and especially loves playing with toys and having a good chew.

Have a resident dog? Set up a meet and greet while you're visiting the shelter.

Check in at the front desk to meet him or if you are offsite, reach out to his advocates online at ClassicCaninesATX or email: caring@classiccanines.org - we aim to make Bandit's transition home an easy one for adopters by helping with a first exam and providing an online gift card for supplies so you can focus on welcoming this sweet guy home.

name: Marcela
description_html: Marcela is a tender soul with a journey full of joy and sorrow. Part of a family for 4 years, her life took a turn that left her feeling lost and alone. Since then she's found places to go, but not her happy, stable place to stay in life,,, nothing has been forever. Most recently she was found alone on IH-130. The Animal Protection Officer who picked her up described her as a "Very sweet girl, just takes a lot of time to warm up". Marcela knows all about abandonment, yet simple kindness, and a sense of calm, and acceptance, is the door to her heart. It doesn't take long to unlock the warmth and affections. She's a great house mate and especially cherishes quiet moments of connection with her people, interlaced with playful moments and toys here and there. Do you have a calm relaxed home for Marcela to feel secure and loved in that you might like to share with her? We invite you to visit her soon or reach out to her advocates at caring@classiccanines.org.

name: Carmelita
description_html:
Meet Carmelita 💕 your future favorite cuddle buddy.


Carmelita is a sweet, affectionate, medium-sized gal (about 50 lbs). She is 100% a cuddle dog and will proudly volunteer as your little spoon any chance she gets. If you’re looking for a loyal companion who loves to cuddle on the couch- She’s your girl.


She’s also a dream in the home:

  • ✅ Fully crate trained — sleeps in her crate overnight for up to 12 hours and goes in willingly during the day


  • ✅ Potty trained — no issues with potty training. She learned to do her business outside by day 2. I had her for 3 weeks and we had no issues with accidents. She typically poops once a day in the morning and does her business IMMEDIATELY when you let her outside. No hemming and hawing there.


  • 🍖 Very food motivated, which makes her eager to learn and train. Will eat absolutely anything you give her and inhales her food, we used a slow feeder.


Carmelita is working on her leash manners and she’s making quick progress! a great sign of her potential with continued guidance and consistency. In our 3 weeks together she progressed very quickly with leash training, with treats and attention distraction she doesn’t bark or have reactivity.


She has the cutest little prance / trot that she does when excited and these adorable low

vocals at meal time (see video).


She is not vocal during the day unless she sees people outside the windows. If she can’t see outside, she’s quite as a mouse.


She doesn’t show any separation anxiety and goes in her crate no problem during the day when you’re leaving. She doesn’t bark in the crate and is a real angel with crate time. Goes right in at bedtime.


I did not have other pets or kids in the home with Carmelita but she sees kids on a walks and greets them and wiggles her butt. She loves kids, I believe she came from a home with kids because she always wanted to greet and interact with children.


I also think she may speak Spanish, lol. She is very attracted to Spanish speaking. I bet she knows commands in Spanish (I do not speak Spanish unfortunately).


I did not get to test her with other dogs but she is playful and curious by nature and not aggressive.

She is obsessed with sniffing and spends half her walk sniffing. She also loves water! (See video).


She knows commands like sit and wait (see video). She will patiently wait in her crate while you feed her meals until you “release” her with an “okay”.


I would love to answer any questions you may have about Carmelita. Feel free to email me at cori@alitelaser.com.


Carmelita is ready to find her forever home — and her forever couch. 🐾💖

name: Courage
description_html:
Courage is a sweet, sensitive boy with a heart full of love—he’s just waiting for the right person to help him feel safe enough to share it. Shelter life is overwhelming for him, and he’s understandably scared in this noisy, unfamiliar environment. What he needs most is someone patient, kind, and willing to give him a little space to come out of his shell at his own pace.

When you let Courage approach you on his terms, he shows you who he truly is: a gentle treat‑taker, a soft hand‑kisser, and a dog who wants so badly to trust. He moves carefully through the world, taking time to understand his surroundings, but every small step he takes is full of bravery.

He walks politely on a leash and stays close, though sudden noises and quick movements can startle him. With time, consistency, and a calm home, he’s sure to blossom into the cuddle bug he’s meant to be.

Courage has the most soulful eyes—eyes that quietly ask for kindness, patience, and love. You can see how much he wants to bond, to feel safe, and to finally relax into a family of his own.

If you’re looking for a gentle companion who will reward your patience with deep, loyal love, Courage is waiting for you.

name: Noelia
description_html: Noelia recently arrived into foster care. More details to come on this sweet girl 💗

name: Dolce
description_html:
Meet Dolce, an incredibly happy lady with a constant helicopter tail who is always ready to get in the mix of friends and family. Her energy level is adjustable, which means she is equally happy to nap on your lap or go on a 2-hour hike. Despite being seven, Dolce indulges in the world with an infectious puppy energy. She loves her occasional zoomies, grass rolls, back scratches, and 20 minutes later, you might find her curled up for a nap. See her short video here! Dolce does not seem bothered by small animals that make their home nearby, including squirrels, birds, and even stray cats.


From her foster parents: Dolce is a dream doggo that seems almost too good to be true. Being the very trusting and loving bundle of joy that she is, Dolce is a perfect embodiment of her name and warmed up to us with belly rubs just within our first few minutes of meeting her. She is extremely affectionate and is almost guaranteed to snuggle right next to you and politely request scratches if you sit down at her level.


Dolce is extremely well-trained (house-, potty-, crate-, leash-trained!) and responds very well to commands like 'sit', 'leave it', 'come here', and 'out'. She also has not had a single accident since joining our home. She appears to be familiar with apartment environments and is confident with stairs and elevators. Dolce also has not reacted to any person, animal, or object (including a guy biking by with a broom!) and responds very well to redirections (verbal commands or tugging the leash). This also means she is an absolute dream on the leash (no pulling at all). Dolce is a classic gentle giant - picking up treats carefully from our hands and nudging her head into our hands for more pets. She is also crate-trained, and demonstrated this on her first night by heading straight to her crate the moment we brought her into our home and consistently sleeping/napping in there.


Dolce is a little overweight as of early Jan 2026, but we are working on lots of exercise and a balanced diet to get her back to her healthy self (and relieve some pressure from those joints!). She is already showing promising signs - she aced long walks and short runs with amazing energy, and has already gotten used to being hand-fed a healthy amount of food throughout the day.


If you are interested in sweet Dolce, please reach out for a meet and greet! We know Dolce would be 10000% thrilled to see and get to know you, and you will get to see her almost fly away with her tail! :)


More short videos of Dolce that showcase her personality:

Dolce showing off her sitting ability!

Dolce making conversation!

Dolce CANNOT wait to go on a walk! (and she's a dream on the leash!)

name: Nellie
description_html:
Nellie is the sweetest and most affectionate little girl you've ever met!


She loves snuggles and pets and is always very happy to sit or nap next to her humans, though she can also be independent and go nap on her bed if they're busy.


She loves playing fetch, she loves her toys and won't turn down a tug of war with them (and will gently place it back in your hand every time she wins so you can keep playing... ADORABLE), and seems very attentive to commands and cues.


She is house trained and can be left alone in the home. Every time her humans come back, they find her napping quietly on her bed.


Nellie knows sit and is enthusiastically learning other tricks. She responds quickly to recall in the yard, but haven't tried it yet out in an unfenced area. She's a pretty good walker, she can sometimes pull a bit when she gets excited, but is learning and improving every day. She doesn't bark or show any aggression toward other dogs we pass on our walks.


If you're looking for a loving and gentle companion who will fill your days with sweet looks and dog kisses, Nellie is your girl!

name: Pebbles
description_html:
Sweet Pebbles is at the head of his class as one of the easiest and nicely mannered dogs in the shelter. So the next step in his plan was to send him to a foster home to collect home behavior intel to help match him to a loving adopter.


He's a dream on a leash - a caregiver that can safely manage a leash will be able to manage Pebbles.

In the home he's fully house trained for potty breaks on walks, he's quiet and well mannered.

Pebbles is just having his first few days in foster so click on his link if you would like a more updated view from his caregiver or to request a meet up.

name: daisy
description_html:
Thelma was found running in the busy road right outside of the shelter. Thankfully she was caught before anything happened to her, and she is now at Austin Animal Services!


Thelma may give a few barks when you walk by her kennel but once that gate is open she can’t get into your lap fast enough! If you are looking for a cuddle bug then this is your girl!


💛 Sweetheart

💛 Cuddly

💛 Playful

💛 Great on Leash

💛 Treat Motivated

💛 Volunteer Favorite

name: Chili Pepper
description_html:
Chili Pepper has a lot of feelings about the shelter, and they are all very mixed! Sometimes she likes us, sometimes she doesn’t. Sometimes she wants pets, sometimes she wants us to go away. The shelter can have this effect on smart, sassy girls like Chili Pepper. This girl is unique because she has thumbs! We think what she really needs is a home to relax in away from the stress of the shelter and other cats. She enjoys pets in moderation here, but she would probably be a super affectionate girl in a calm home setting. She’s just telling us she deserves better than a kennel!


In her foster home, she loves being where her people are! She’s very interested in what everyone is up to, and Chili Pepper is constantly rubbing on her company to show her affection. Her favorite spots include the corner of the couch and any bathroom countertop she can get ahold of (Chili Pepper LOVES the sinks. It’s an odd preference for sure. See photo evidence).

Chili Pepper is easy going and very lovable. We’d love to see her land a home and get to share her little personality!

name: Scout
description_html: Scout is a striking 6yo tuxedo cat who is a true companion at heart. She is a social girl who loves nothing more than being around her people and will often be found curled up right on the bed for a long nap. Scout is an expert at finding the coziest hiding spots for a deep sleep, but she also has a fun, energetic side that comes out during her nightly zoomies. She is incredibly easy to please, showing enthusiasm for every toy she finds and excitement for any treat offered to her. Scout tells us she prefers to be the only pet in the home so that she can be adored for all her unique qualities!

name: Oso
description_html: Oso has the kind of soul that quietly steals your heart and never gives it back. At 4 years old, he is gentle, deeply loving, and just wants to belong to a family of his own. He melts into cuddles, leans into love, and looks at you like you’re his whole world. Oso is wonderful with kids, adores other dogs, and greets everyone he meets with warmth and trust. When the weather turns cold, he happily snuggles up in his little jammies, perfectly content just being close. Oso isn’t just looking for a home—he’s waiting for his people, the ones he can love forever. If you meet him, you’ll understand why he’s impossible to forget. 💙🐾

name: Indy
description_html:
Meet Indy – Your Future Adventure Buddy & Professional Cuddle Companion! 🐾

Indy is a smart, playful girl with a big heart and an even bigger love for her people. She truly believes everyone she meets is her new best friend — and she’s not wrong. 💛


This bright girl is always ready to run and wrestle with her dog friends! She was in a foster home with a 9-year-old Mini Aussie and a 2-year-old Lab and loved every minute of playtime. Indy shows zero aggression and would absolutely thrive in a home with another young, playful dog to help burn off her energy.


When the fun winds down, Indy transforms into the ultimate cuddle bug. She loves to curl up behind you while you nap and is happiest when she’s close to her person. If left alone, she may cry a little — she just really loves company! With her people, though, she’s calm, quiet, and affectionate.


Indy is incredibly smart and picks up on new things quickly. She enjoys puzzle toys, Kongs, durable chew toys, and bones — especially the indestructible kind! Mealtime is serious business for Indy; she eats dry food and inhaled it, so a slow feeder works great to help her pace herself.


She’s still working on her house training and will need consistency and patience as she continues learning. Frequent potty breaks (about every 30 minutes when someone is home) have helped her succeed, but she may have accidents if left alone too long. Indy is also not a fan of crates and has proven she’s quite the escape artist, so a home prepared to work on positive crate training — or explore alternative management solutions — will be important.


On leash, Indy doesn’t pull but is easily distracted by the exciting world around her. With her quick mind and eagerness to learn, she has great potential to become a wonderful walking partner.


Indy’s Ideal Home:

  • Another young, playful dog
  • Active adults who can provide structure and training
  • Someone home often (she loves companionship!)
  • Plenty of durable toys and enrichment


If you’re looking for a playful best friend who will keep you laughing, learning, and snuggling, Indy might just be your girl. 🐶💕

name: River
description_html: River showed up at the home of a nice person who gave her a chance indoors and she blossomed. While she may be shy in a kennel, she is lovable and affectionate in the home. She has a cute little void face and big eyes that blink slowly when we chat with her. She may do well with a cat friend (as most cats who come from outdoors do). River is looking for a calm place to relax and show her true self.

name: Victoria
description_html:
Cuddly, sweet, a good listener, respectful, a good learner - all these things describe Victoria.

She's never over the top, she's just right. She's stunning inside and out.


Like all very young dogs, she's perfectly moldable and tailorable with a little guidance from her very important people. .


Stop by so she can charm you.

name: Yuji
description_html: Shhh,,real quiet like, my name is Yuji and I'm the shy one around here. I let my best friend Amy take the lead and I choose to stay in the background, sometimes trying not to be noticed. But when it's time for walks and treats I'm all in, don't skip me. Amy is the social butterfly so I let her get the people attention, she needs it. Me? I'm pretty content and easy to please, no fan fare needed. And conflict? Not for me. If you like taking strolls and need a good reason to meet neighbors and friends, I'm your girl and so is my social butterfly friend Amy ID 20577 - she likes to steal the show, she's a people magnet. If you're open to making us your dog family, it would be a true delight to stay together. We'll keep each other company when you're not home. We're easy to walk together too. We won't let you down!

name: Rusty
description_html:
Meet Rusty 💛

Rusty is a joyful, fun-loving pup who's ready to find his forever family! With his sweet nature and boundless energy, he brings happiness everywhere he goes. He loves being part of the action-whether that's playing fetch in the yard, going for neighborhood walks, or simply curling up near his favorite people after a day of fun.

Rusty thrives on attention and companionship. He's happiest when he's included and involved, making him a wonderful match for an active family or someone looking for a loyal best friend by their side.

The best part? Rusty already has a great foundation! He's potty trained, crate trained, and knows his basic commands. He's smart, eager to please, and loves learning new things-so continued training will be both easy and fun.

If you're looking for a loving, playful, and devoted companion who will greet you with a wagging tail and endless affection, Rusty could be the perfect addition to your home. He's ready to give his whole heart to his forever family-could that be you? 🐾💕

name: Leorio
description_html:
What a sweet guy to meet and get to know - you must put him on your list. Although he's young and a little shy, he's calm and shows nice manners and nice boundary control. He's affectionate too. We notice he's not one to jump on benches and furniture without being invited. He loves the close proximity of companionship with people, and we wouldn't be surprised if that extended to dog friends too.

Have a resident dog? You can setup a meet and greet after meeting him make sure it's a great mutual fit.

Ask for help getting him out of his kennel.

name: Vittorio
description_html: Vittorio is a compact model of love and charm that comes equipped with extra peripheral vision. He knows a thing or two about keeping a tidy house, as he keeps his kennel clean and seems house trained. While he may look worried, that's just him waiting to see if his attempts at telepathy are working--he wants to read your mind, share his own ideas with you, and make you happy! He can't wait to show you he's the goodest boy who loves back scratches, playing fetch, and walks with you on the other end of his leash!

name: Millie
description_html:
Millie arrived with 2 other dogs from home. She was lucky enough to get to a foster home where she's getting her medical needs caught up while having the lap time she craves. Millie co-exists well with small dogs, but also doesn't require another dog to feel whole. It's her humans that she chooses as her very best friends.


Millie is very housetrained and a nice walker on leash.


Millie's medical catch up is being sponsored by ClassicCaninesATX

She's expected to be fully ready for adoption in April but that doesn't stop you from inquiring and meeting her now. Reply to her link.to get the conversation started!

name: The Duchess Of Purr
description_html:
The Duchess was in foster care and this is what her foster had to say about her!


"She’s the sweetest. She’s extremely in tune with people. She loves nibbling on fingers and toes but will respect boundaries. Loves to party at 3 am. Purrs like a symphony. Will attack the vacuum. 10/10 lovely cat and deserves a loving home."


This girl is pretty scared at the shelter, so we would love to see her back in a home ASAP! Adopt The Duchess of Purr today and give her a stress-free life in a loving home.

name: Patty Mayonnaise
description_html: Patty Mayonnaise is the perfect polar bear to add to your family! She loves people and affection but isn’t over-bearing (pun intended). She has had a puppy roommate at the shelter and been in foster before with other dogs so bring down your resident dog for a meet and greet to make the perfect match! She may have some words for her fellow playmates and the squirrels but her sweet nature can’t be beat. Patty is looking for someone to take a chance on her and let her live out her life being the curious dog she is.

name: Bronx
description_html:
Can you imagine how scared a gentle giant must have been to be found dumped in a public bathroom?


Bronx deserves more than to be judged by his size, his look, or his breed. He deserves someone who sees the resilience in him, the softness in him, and the dog he is still becoming. Right now, he is far too skinny, and it is clear life has not always been kind to him, but Bronx is still here, still trying, and still full of heart.


He may be a very large dog, but at his core Bronx is a gentle marshmallow in a giant dog body. He is sweet, goofy, playful, and just a little dramatic in the charming way. He can be a little hand shy when first meeting someone and likes a moment to make sure he is safe, but once he knows you, he is deeply loyal and devoted. 


Bronx is the kind of dog who stays close, leans in for pets, and looks at you with those big soulful eyes like he has been waiting for you. Despite his size, he is gentle with his love. He does not want to bowl you over. He just wants to be near you, learn with you, and be a little team together. He is house trained in kennel, leash trained, laid back, food motivated, and playful, which gives him such a strong foundation for the person willing to give him a real, stable chance. 


What Bronx needs now is someone patient and kind. Someone who will choose him not because he looks impressive, but because they understand what it means to help a dog come back into himself. Give Bronx a little time, some good meals, and a safe place to land, and you may just end up with the most loyal sidekick of all.

name: Lucius
description_html:
Meet Lucius! 🐾

Lucius is a sweet, smiley boy with a tender heart and gentle nature. He’s housetrained, walks beautifully on leash, and is very treat-motivated—making him an easy companion to spend time with.

A little shy at first, Lucius can be timid in new situations and may pull when he feels unsure, but he warms up with patience and kindness. Once comfortable, his soft personality and quiet sweetness really shine.

If you’re looking for a gentle, sensitive pup who just needs a little confidence and a lot of love, Lucius could be your special guy.

name: Cupid
description_html: Prepare to fall in love with Cupid! Don't say we didn't warn you about this lovable guy. He craves affection and will stick close to you on outings. Cupid is an excellent walker on leash, and though the shelter may be overwhelming for him, we think he could really flourish in a quiet space with patient, loving people to get him back on track. Just in time for Valentines, meet the new love of your life today with Cupid!

name: Beefy
description_html: Meet Beefy! A gentleman with a heart of gold and a personality to match! This lovable goofball is all smiles and wiggles, and he lives for playtime and making new friends. Beefy would thrive with someone who can work on his enthusiastic leash manners, he's excited to see the world and be the most rewarding companion.

name: Darnell
description_html:
Darnel is as big-hearted as he is big-eared! 

 

This playful, silly boy has lots of energy and loves toys, treats, and a good game of fetch. Darnell prefers to be the only dog so he can soak up all the attention, and once he feels comfortable, his affectionate side really shines. 

 

Darnell just needs a patient person who will let him warm up at his own pace. Give him that chance, and you'll gain a loyal companion. 

 

Think Darnell might be your guy? Come meet this big-eared goofball!

name: Helios
description_html:
This sweet boy is the perfect mix of playful and affectionate! He has medium energy and would thrive in an active home where he can enjoy walks, playtime, and plenty of love.

He walks nicely on leash with a harness and enjoys getting out for strolls, spending time in the play yard, or exploring during outings. One of his favorite things in the world is a tennis ball—he loves a good game of fetch and will happily bring the ball back, either dropping it at your feet or gently letting you take it from him.

When playtime is over, he’s all about the cuddles. He’s a total snuggler who loves belly rubs and being close to his people. He’s also very gentle, enjoys treats, and already knows the sit command—making training fun and rewarding.

He appears to be housetrained, is leash trained, and seems calm when passing other dogs or people on walks. With his playful spirit, loving personality, and fondness for treats, he’s ready to bring lots of joy to his future family!

name: Oliver Sebastian
description_html:
Oliver Sebastian is an affectionate and cuddly guy who can get anxious when he's feeling uncertain about things. The shelter is a tough place for an anxious kitty, so we would love to see this boy in a home ASAP. He tends to bond strongly with his person and prefers to be the only baby in the family. This senior guy would love a break from kennel life, either through adoption or foster.

I am a DHC, and I have my own webpage!

The adoption fee is waived for Oliver Sebastian and ALL Desperate Housecats!

name: Bentley
description_html:
We’ll never understand how someone could leave Bentley outside during the winter freeze without food, water, or shelter. What we do know is that he’s safe now and showing everyone just what an incredible dog he is.


While Bentley is protected here, what he truly needs is a loving forever family who will show him the unconditional love he deserves.


Bentley is fun, curious, and incredibly affectionate. He has a way of making people laugh with his playful personality, and he’s just as happy to be by your side offering comfort when you need it most. He’s the kind of dog who quickly becomes your best friend.


And let’s not forget, Bentley is basically a dog model! With his good looks and charming personality, he’s sure to turn heads wherever he goes.


Bentley is ready to start his new life filled with love, warmth, and security. Could you be his chance at the forever home he’s been waiting for?

name: Jinx
description_html:
Meet Jinx! 🐾

Jinx is an absolute angel with a playful spirit and so much love to give. She’s very housetrained and happiest when she’s out in the play yard, soaking up fun and fresh air.

She even knows the cutest trick—“sit pretty”—and it’s guaranteed to make you smile every time. Sweet, lively, and full of personality, Jinx is ready for a home that will adore her just as much as she’ll adore them.

name: Oliver
description_html:
Oliver came to us off the street in South Austin. He is such a sweet guy and has been easy going from the day we brought him in. Hes very laid back but still enjoys getting out on walks. He gets along great with our dogs and the neighbors. He has also been great with cats. He hasnt been around any children yet but I dont see that being an issue.


He know a few commands and is easily treat motivated. I have taken him on a few car rides to adventure around and he is super chill in the car and lays down in the back seat.


Oliver is a medium energy level dog. He loves taking long walks but when hes at home hes very content to lay in his bed and nap.


I can see him fitting into almost anyones home.

name: Mocha
description_html:
Mocha is a sweet, affectionate young mom who is discovering just how nice indoor life can be.


Mocha was originally a community cat before coming to AAC in early February. Since then she has quickly learned that life indoors means cozy window naps, kind humans, and lots of love. She is incredibly affectionate, enjoys being held, and loves to cuddle. Instead of loud meows, Mocha communicates with the cutest little chirps.


Mocha is still figuring out toys since she didn’t grow up playing with them, but she’s curious and learning more every day. She is fully litter box trained and has quickly adapted to being an indoor house cat.


She’s friendly with visitors and has done well with a laid-back resident cat in her foster home. She may enjoy a calm cat companion rather than a very high-energy one. She has not been around dogs.


Mocha recently finished raising her adorable kitten, Bean. She has been a great mom, but now that Bean is growing up she’s ready to find a home of her own where she can soak up all the love she deserves.

name: Mustang
description_html:
Chevy & Mustang were born and raised at an auto body shop. Unfortunately, the property became unsafe for them and that's when they came into our care. These are not house cats looking for couches or cuddles. They are outdoor cats looking for a job and they are ready to work for you!

Working cats still rely on people for food, water, and appropriate shelter — they just prefer not to be pet or handled. In return, they provide natural pest control and thrive in an environment that matches their comfort level.

We’re hoping to find a safe, appropriate working cat placement where they can settle in and thrive. They will be spayed/neutered, fully vaccinated, and ear-tipped prior to placement. Our Community Cats team will help with humane relocation!

Interested in 24/7 private pest control? Email communitycats@austintexas.gov.

I am a DHC, and I have my own webpage!

The adoption fee is waived for Mustang and ALL Cactus Cats/Desperate Housecats!

name: Cicero
description_html:
Cicero is the kind of dog who steals hearts without even trying. With his gentle spirit and soulful eyes, it’s truly impossible not to fall in love with him the moment you meet him.


This sweet boy loves going on walks and has beautiful leash manners. Cicero is also a big fan of ear scratches—give him a few and you’ll see his whole face light up with pure happiness.


Cicero is super house trained, sits for treats, and his calm demeanor has made him a volunteer's favorite.


If you’re looking for a loyal, loving companion who will bring warmth and peaceful energy into your home, Cicero might just be your perfect match. 🐾💛

name: Darcy
description_html: Darcy is a lovely lady who would do best as the only pet in the home. She wants to be the queen of the house and get all the attention! She also has no problem setting boundaries and letting you know when she's ready for some time to herself.

name: Cupcake
description_html: As a free-spirited, cheerful soul who enjoys her independence. She rides well in the car and loves adventure walks. She develops deep bonds with her person, following them dutifully around the house. She loves having her own stash of squeaky toys. She's described as hilarious, sweet and smart. Cupcake knows sit, stay and down and is housetrained. When she's done being a Jr Explorer, she appreciates naps in a spot all her own, where ever she won't be bothered by others encroaching in on her spot or she will protest! She may choose to be by her person. She does best as a solo dog in a calm adult home, but also did well in a limited time setting with an aloof, independent dog friend that didn't compete for affections and sleep spots. Cupcake is charming indeed, yet doesn't like being hastily jostled around by dogs and children, or having to compete with others for access to her very important people. Our sweet tenderoni wants you all to herself. Looking for a personal companion?

name: Kya
description_html:
Kya is a sweet, playful companion who loves walks and loves to be interactive with people - she's part diva and part funny ham.

She's still learning the ropes of being in a busy, very public kennel building- it can be downright scary for new arriving dogs, so if she steps outside for a moment when you're near, don't take offense-ask for help to meet her instead and you'll get to meet the real Kya, plus maybe you've save her the expense of having to endure more time in a shelter.

She does her best to keep a neat, tidy kennel while walks come just once or twice daily. She's spayed, vaccinated, and heart worm negative and ready to leave right away.

Her advocates at ClassicCaninesATX have adoption support specials too-ask staff about them or reach out to caring@classiccanines.org

name: Cinna
description_html:
Cinna was being well cared for, but she was mostly living outdoors. This gorgeous feline is now ready to have a permanent indoor home. Her owner described her as “very sweet, but sometimes easily frightened”.

Cinna is a member of our Cactus Cats, and is learning that people are good to have around. She’s not comfortable in the shelter environment, but once she is in a real home, with plenty of time to acclimate, she will be very friendly and affectionate. She’ll even let you hold her, and she likes being brushed.

Cinna lived with other cats and small dogs and will likely be happiest with a buddy.

I am a DHC, and I have my own webpage!

The adoption fee is waived for Cinna and ALL Cactus Cats/Desperate Housecats!

name: Dawn
description_html:
Dawn and her sister were found in a field and were rescued by a good Samaritan. Both kitties were very shy at first, although Dawn's sister warmed up pretty quickly and has been adopted.

Dawn is still learning to trust, and she tends to hide, but she loves being petted when a friendly hand gently encourages her. She will benefit from being in a quiet home with a cat buddy who can help her build her confidence with humans.

name: Pearl
description_html: Sweet girl Pearl is a lively little cutie looking for her forever playmates! She is partly housetrained, loves to play in the yard and is starting to play fetch!! Pearly girl is sassy and will let you know with a little bark when she wants her food! Pearl lives with other dogs but could also live with cats or kids as well. This spunky little pup is ready to hit the town with her fam!! Could she be your dreamgirl?

name: Dottie
description_html: Adorable Dottie has finished her puppy shots and got spayed and now she's ready to find her forever family! Dottie is smart and will sit pretty for her food. She loves frozen kongs, snuggles and playing tug of war. She is great with her big foster brothers and would do well as an only dog or as part of a pack. This little adventurer is ready to explore Austin with you!!

name: Barbie
description_html: Barbie is a gentle, soft natured girl who loves to cuddle. She enjoys gentle head pets and appreciates Temptations cat treats. She had to be surrendered due to life situation changing. Barbie may be shy in the kennel, but with a little encouragement she's an absolute snuggle bug.

name: Jezebel
description_html: Jezebel is a free spirit who's looking for a way out of this kennel! She would love a home with some windows to watch the world outside or a safe indoor/outdoor setting. Jezebel may be prone to overstimulation so she needs a cat savvy person who understands this behavior and can give her guidance and support. She loves to play and this is a great way to satisfy this active girl's needs. Jezebel is very loving and enjoys pets and lap cuddles. She's lived with dogs and may do well with a pet companion in the home.

name: Barkus
description_html:
Barkus was found wandering the streets and eager to greet each dog he crossed. We don’t know his story, but he came in rough shape but a happy and gentle heart.


He gets along great with his foster sister(pit mix) and brother (chihuahua mix). He enjoys running and playing with his foster sister (though he can be a bit rough when very excited). He seems to like to please, sitting right away for treats and giving his paw for more pets. He knows sit, paw, and lays down in the car before exiting. What a good boy!


He is crate trained, walking into his crate with no hesitation and no fuss. I work from home and he doesn’t mind just laying around (free roam) until my work day is over.


Barkus enjoys chewing on ice, sunbathing, chasing lizards, playing fetch, toys and car rides. Did not bark when strangers entered the yard, just comes closely for pets. We’re still getting the hang of walking on a leash and he seems to be leash reactive with dogs, but I think he just really wants to say hello to other dogs.


He will require slow introductions to children as he doesn’t seem to understand them yet. I’m confident that he’ll warm up quickly, once he understands he’s safe.


Barkus seems to just want to be loved and is the sweetest boy around.

name: Chili
description_html: Chili is one of many cats who were found abandoned in a residence. She is shy and unsure of the shelter, which is completely natural given all that he's been through. Chili and all of her feline friends may need some reassurance in a new place and some time to get used to new people. They are all looking for a loving home where they can relax and show their true personalities. They may do well in a space with other cats or can go home together.

name: Slinky
description_html: Slinky came in with several other cats after being abandoned in a residence. It's tough for most pets to adapt to the shelter environment, but especially for those who have been through extreme hardship. Slinky and his friends are looking for a calm place with a cat savvy person to help them get used to their new lives. They may do best with friendly cats in the home (or one of their buddies) to guide them and offer comfort.

name: Clove
description_html: Clove is one of many cats who were found abandoned in a residence. He is shy and unsure of the shelter, which is completely natural given all that he's been through. Clove and all of his feline friends may need some reassurance in a new place and some time to get used to new people. They are all looking for a loving home where they can relax and show their true personalities.

name: Stitch
description_html: Stitch came in with several other cats after being abandoned in a residence. It's tough for most pets to adapt to the shelter environment, but especially for those who have been through extreme hardship. Stitch and his friends are looking for a calm place with a cat savvy person to help them get used to their new lives. They may do best with friendly cats in the home (or one of their buddies) to guide them and offer comfort.

name: Nino
description_html:
Nino is a showstopper with one bright blue eye and one warm brown eye—truly one of a kind!

 

Once he’s out and about, Nino is a laid-back, happy boy with a constantly wagging tail. He’s a mostly loose-leash walker who loves sniffing on walks and showing off his good manners—he even knows commands like check in and sit! Nino is house trained and can be very affectionate. He loves to play with toys, especially any rope toy he can find!

 

Nino does best with slow introductions and prefers to be outside of his kennel. With a little patience, this unique-eyed boy quickly becomes a loving partner. 

 

Ready to fall for those unforgettable eyes? Come meet Nino!

name: Butters
description_html:
Butters arrived with an active much smaller sized dog friend who since found a new home. Butters is waiting for his next adventure in life while sharing his kennel space with another large dog. He loves being part of all the activity going on inside and out, and seems perfectly suited for an active lifestyle. He's not ready to slow down any time soon. He'll charm you to the moon and back, along with all your friends too as a great house and walk companion.


Butters and his original doggie accomplice had a mishap with chickens before arriving to the shelter. Expect that Butters may treat small mammals as prey or sport and his home should have a well secured fence.

name: Sassy
description_html:
Sassy is a stunning all-black beauty with a bright white chest and an adorable smile that makes you look back twice.

She is a medium energy girlie, which is perfect for fun, playful moments and then settling down for a relaxing, laid-back rest afterward.

She may be a little shy and timid at first, but with a little patience (and a few treats) Sassy’s sweet side quickly shines through. She’s affectionate, playful, enjoys checking in with her person, and walks nicely on leash. An active home with a yard or space to explore would be perfect for her.

If you’re ready to help a shy girl gain confidence, Sassy might just be your most loyal adventure buddy.

name: Billie
description_html:
Billie is slowly getting used to the shelter life and her personality is starting to shine through!


Although she is shy at first, she is opening up to people and enjoying a few pets from trusted friends.


She’s got a playful side to her, and will play a few rounds of fetch before taking a snooze break in our mock living room set ups.


Come meet Billie today!

name: Strudel
description_html: Strudel came in with several other cats from a difficult situation. Despite this, she's affectionate and learning to be braver each day. She may be found in a hiding spot, but she will gladly accept pets and ask for more if you are patient with her. She loves Churu so that may be a great way to make friends. Strudel may enjoy a friendly cat companion in her new home or the company of one of her mates.

name: Kola
description_html:
Kola is a majestic 6-year-old guy with a soft heart and an easygoing spirit. After recently losing his person, he is now hoping for a gentle fresh start with someone who will give him the love he deserves. He may be a big, fluffy boy, but his vibe is easy, breezy, and wonderfully laid back.


Kola is sweet, cuddly, potty trained in kennel, and leash trained, and he has the kind of calm presence that makes being around him feel easy. He is affectionate without being too much, relaxed without being distant, and easy to read in a way that makes it simple to understand what he is feeling and what helps him happy.


If you are looking for a mellow, loving companion with a majestic coat and an even sweeter soul, Kola may be your guy. He has been through a lot, and now he deserves a soft landing, a steady person, and all the love in the world.

name: Milo
description_html:
Milo was once lost but now has found a temporary home at the shelter.

His finder reported he's calm and friendly

Staff reported he's friendly, sweet, gentle, and very calm

In addition he has nice leash manners


He's an easy relaxing boy without a care in the world who simply enjoys the companionship of his people and having a cozy blanket to curl up on. His motto: "Life is good".


Be the change making a difference in his life by bringing him home. He's advocated for by his friends at caring@classiccanines.org - stop by to meet him or email with questions.

name: Milo
description_html:
Meet Milo ~

A sweet gentleman, always wearing a smile, mostly non-sighted, possibly due to Uveodermatologic Syndrome (UDS), a condition affecting breeds like his. He's fully content, enjoying life, using all his sensory functions.What to expect when meeting Milo? He'll lean and nuzzle into you, rolling out the welcome wagon..what a nice welcome for your friends and family who are coming over...hold the treats, he doesn't eat them,, but do look at his perfect teeth, no tartar-impressive! He's heart worm negative, a perfect weight. His coat is beauuuutiful and glorious.

He walks great on a leash and he'll show you how his nose, his side-to-side walk motion, and how airflow keeps him on a steady track. He can also climb a few patio stairs. He recently lived well with a cat! He's a stand out in a crowd and low vision is not a hardship to him, he adapted well. Here's the sweetheart you didn't know you needed,,until you meet him.

name: Honey Bunny
description_html: Honey Bunny is a sweet girl who loves to play and nap. She is house trained and great around kids. Currently, she is in a house with an 8 year old and no other animals. She is very friendly with our neighbor's rottweiler and does well on walks. She loves to play and will thrive in house with a yard or somewhere she can go on walks, but also does well in her kennel during the day. I have not had any issues with her being aggressive around food, but she eats fast. I will be introducing her to a slow feeder, but I have seen no issues with her digestion etc. I have bathed her and even trimmed her nails with no fuss from her. She would be a great fit in any loving home!

name: Chevy
description_html:
Chevy & Mustang were born and raised at an auto body shop. Unfortunately, the property became unsafe for them and that's when they came into our care. These are not house cats looking for couches or cuddles. They are outdoor cats looking for a job and they are ready to work for you!

Working cats still rely on people for food, water, and appropriate shelter — they just prefer not to be pet or handled. In return, they provide natural pest control and thrive in an environment that matches their comfort level.

We’re hoping to find a safe, appropriate working cat placement where they can settle in and thrive. They will be spayed/neutered, fully vaccinated, and ear-tipped prior to placement. Our Community Cats team will help with humane relocation!

Interested in 24/7 private pest control? Email communitycats@austintexas.gov.

I am a DHC, and I have my own webpage!

The adoption fee is waived for Chevy and ALL Cactus Cats/Desperate Housecats!

name: Juno
description_html: Juno may be scared in the shelter, but under normal circumstances, he has a LOT of love to give. He loves to be scratched on the top of his head, he will trust you with belly rubs, and you might often find him snuggled between your legs or against your back in the morning. Juno's hobbies include biscuit making, playing in cardboard boxes, window-watching, napping in a sunbeam and he enjoys a running faucet. He loves to play and is overall just a happy boy who craves attention but will let you know when he's had enough. Juno makes for a loyal friend to whoever takes good care of him!

name: Bucky
description_html: Bucky is a shy little guy who was brought in by one of our amazing TNR volunteers in February! She was assisting an overwhelmed community cat caregiver with getting her colony of cats spayed or neutered when she came across this little one! He was only 4 months old when he arrived and our volunteer noticed right away he was little and might have a chance at finding an indoor home! Our fantastic Cat Socialization volunteers have started working their magic with him. At first, he was very afraid and skittish. He has made so much progress thanks to our volunteers' dedication and patience and, of course, thanks to his love of Churu treats! Even though he initially comes across as a little spicy and hissy, he relaxes with a calm, gentle approach, a little Churu, and a straw/string toy to help break the ice. This kitty would likely benefit from an older, friendly or likewise calm kitty in the home to help build his confidence. A quiet home with older kiddos or adults only will likely lead him to success and though it may take a bit longer (a few weeks to months) to settle in and become comfortable, he deserves it and will repay you with a lifetime of love and trust!

name: Bolt
description_html:
Meet Bolt: a happy, silly, and playful boy who’s discovering just how wonderful the world can be.


Bolt loves spending time with his people and finds comfort simply being by your side. He’s always up for a great game of fetch and would make an amazing adventure buddy.


After a day of fun, Bolt is just as happy to settle in and show his affectionate side. He has so much love to give and enjoys being close to the people he trusts. He’s also very house-trained, making him an easy addition to a home.


With his unique personality and joyful spirit, Bolt truly is a special dog. All he needs now is a forever family to share life’s adventures with.


Could that be you?


--

name: Antonelli
description_html:
A sweet boy with all the puppy looks and charms arrived with a large case of dogs and puppies from the same location, facing a brave new world ahead. His name has been perfectly matched to his play yard speed. Playing and running have been a great release for the worries of being in a scary, noisy shelter. He absolutely adores all the soft toys too.

Antonelli has been entertaining us and planting little kisses while he waits for his humans to guide him in his new life ahead. He has years to give and wants to be part of, and learn from, a loving, fun home crew.

name: Velveteen
description_html:
Meet Velveteen ~

Here is the sweetest, gentlest, quietest lady in the room. She has distinctive features like a bunny hop in her trot, and no tail, earning her a sweet rabbit tale name. She loves to quietly watch life unfold, from a window view, or a patio.


She's spayed, vaccinated and heart worm negative. She's house trained and butter smooth on a leash. She's a great compact size, and combined with her good girl manners, she makes an easy fit in many home environments. She shares her space nicely with a large lady senior dog friend.


Velveteen has secured a foster home for most of March. She is available for adoption, or for a foster takeover. To request a meet up for adoption, apply at her link. To request a foster takeover, be sure to complete a foster application at https://www.austintexas.gov/services/foster-pets and then send email to animal.foster@austintexas.gov referencing 21928 - Velveteen


Velveteen has an advocacy group of volunteers - if you have questions, write us at caring@classiccanines.org

name: Asa
description_html:
"Asa", is a sweet, quiet, mellow fellow with years of wisdom and boyish young looks. He was found at a landscaping center with scratches on his face that beg an untold story, and the truth is he was a little light on the groceries. He's getting a chance now to dial all of that back while in a foster home he shares with a senior lady dog.


He is very agreeable to sweet talk and petting and has a small list of loves like soft blankies, treats, short walks and quiet times enjoying a gentle breeze on the patio. Asa likes to take in life at a slow, casual pace, balancing indoor time with a few patio privileges and is looking for a permanent human partner who is similar minded. Here is your easy gentle friend waiting to be a quiet companion. Reach out on his link to set up a meet. .

name: Dandelion
description_html: Dandelion loves to cuddle! She's very people focused and enjoys having fun with whoever is hanging out with her. She's a playful, friendly girl who was brought in as a stray, but we can tell she had a home at some point. Dandelion is crate trained and mellow once she gets all her wiggles out. This fun girl is ready for a loving home today!

name: Rascal
description_html:
Meet Rascal—your future 60-pound lap dog!


Rascal is ready to leave his past behind and start the next chapter of his life with a loving forever family. This handsome boy is as easy going as they come and has quickly become a volunteer favorite.


Rascal walks beautifully on leash and enjoys relaxed strolls where he can take in the world by your side. He’s a loose-leash walker who makes walks a breeze. While he enjoys getting out and about, what Rascal really loves most is simply being near his people.


With his calm, even-keeled personality and affectionate nature, Rascal is the kind of dog who fits right in and makes every day a little brighter. And did we mention how handsome he is?


Rascal can’t wait to meet his forever family - maybe that’s you!

name: Shrimp
description_html:
Meet the snuggly, spunky, sweet Shrimp! Although he may come off as shy, he warms up quickly to every human he's met (can be easily won over with treats)! This silly puppy is ready to find his forever home! Shrimp is energetic in the home, loves plushie toys he can chew on, and will certainly become your shadow. As much as he absolutely loves to cuddle, he also enjoys getting cozy in his crate. When leaving, he can be coaxed in with treats and settles down immediately!


Shrimp is nearly 100% housebroken, he has had a couple accidents inside, but knows he should go outside to do his business. He enjoys going on walks, but needs an encouraging, patient human who will support him when the outside world feels overstimulating and noisy! When it comes to other pets, Shrimp would prefer to be your one and only! He is still learning that other animals aren't so scary after all.


Are you the one who can help this canine crustacean come out of his shell? Meet the spectacular Shrimp today!!

name: Sigrid
description_html:
Sigrid is a sweet, gentle girl who is easy to love and easy to live with. She enjoys the simple things in life—especially taking her time to sniff the world during walks. She’s a lovely loose-leash walker and truly enjoys exploring at her own pace.

Sigrid is friendly with both people and other dogs, and she’s now happily paired with a kennel mate. Her calm, easygoing nature could make her a wonderful option for first-time pet parents, families, or anyone looking for a relaxed and sweet companion.

She knows sit, shake, rides great in the car, is crate trained, and seems to be potty trained as well.

If you’re looking for a gentle, friendly dog who will happily join you for sniffy walks and sweet companionship, Sigrid is your girl

name: Pixie
description_html:
Pixie is a 28-pound bundle of sweetness looking for a cozy place to call home. This quiet, gentle girl is already showing how quickly she learns—she’s doing great practicing her leash walking and improving every day.


Pixie has a calm, easygoing personality and absolutely loves being close to her people. She happily allows nail trims and handles gentle care like a champ, showing just how trusting and well-mannered she is.


Her favorite thing in the world? Snuggling. Pixie would be thrilled to curl up beside you—or even better, right in your lap—for some quality cuddle time. If you’re looking for a soft-hearted companion who will happily keep you company and shower you with quiet affection, Pixie might be the perfect match.

name: Frankie
description_html: Frankie is an easy going, affectionate girl who arrived at the shelter as a stray. Unfortunately, her microchip was a dead-end. But we know this 10 year old, laid back senior was loved at some point. She's so easy to love, after all! Frankie enjoys pets and hanging out near you. She's ready for a sofa and some popcorn on a rainy day with her best human friends. Adopt Frankie today!

name: Miss Pretty
description_html:
Miss Pretty just arrived on 3/5 and she's been worried to be here, in a new noisy place so her shelter friends are helping her adjust and be comfortable, so she'll be ready to meet you.


What we know so far;

--She'll never turn down a treat

--She loves comfort

--She seems very house trained and she's crate trained

--She walks well on a leash and isn't bothered by other dogs nearby


A meet up with a resident dog can be scheduled while onsite. Check in with staff or write her advocates at caring@classiccanines.org.

name: Sage
description_html:
Are you looking for a playful and sweet girl? Sage might be the dog for you!

Sage loves spending time with volunteer friends in the play yard, where her fun personality really shines. She’s a supreme toy tester and a bit of a ham for the camera — always ready to show off her silly side. Sage still has a wonderfully young spirit and plenty of playful energy. After she gets her initial zoomies out, she’s happy to settle into a nice walk and enjoy the world around her. 

You might hear Sage chatting in her kennel, but she’s just trying to tell you hello — and maybe ask if you’ll take her home. Sage is friendly with people and has lived with other dogs before, too. We can host a doggie meetup with yours and see how the dogs hit it off.

This comeback princess has left her past behind and is ready for a life filled with love, comfort, and new adventures. Ask staff for help to meet her or if offsite reach out to her advocates at caring@classiccanines.org.

name: Eloise
description_html:
Are you looking for a world-class cuddler who will absolutely melt your heart? Then Eloise might just be your perfect match.


After leaving a past of cruelty behind her, Eloise is ready to start the happy chapter she truly deserves with a loving forever family by her side. Despite everything she’s been through, her heart is full of love and trust. She’s incredibly affectionate and happiest when she’s giving gentle hugs or snuggling up close to her favorite people.


Eloise also has a playful side and loves spending time with her beloved stuffies. She’s the perfect size, wonderfully gentle, and has such a kind, sweet spirit that everyone who meets her falls for her instantly. She also gets along well with other dogs.


Eloise has already stolen the hearts of many volunteers, and we’re pretty sure she’ll steal yours too. All she needs now is a safe, loving home where she can continue to blossom and share all the love she has to give.

name: Udon
description_html: Udon came in with his litter mates from the community. He's a friendly guy once he settles into a new situation. Udon loves to play and enjoys attention from people. He may like having some cat friends to romp around with as well!

name: Fedora
description_html: Fedora has been on this green earth for 17 years, so she's earned her retirement. She's used to running her household as she sees fit, and now suddenly she's stuck in a kennel, which can be a tough change to accept. Fedora did not appreciate the company of the toddler in the home, but that's no crime. This senior girl is looking for a calm place where she can enjoy some uninterrupted window watching and get some attention and pets when she's in the mood for it. She's available for foster or adoption today.

name: Snowball
description_html:
Snowball is the sweetest boy and all about the cozy life. He’s a gentle snuggler who loves curling up next to his people and soaking up all the pets or belly rubs that he can get. If there’s a comfy spot nearby, he’ll happily settle in and keep you company.


He has a calm, easygoing personality and does well with his other big dog friends. He’s not the rough-and-tumble type and would much rather lounge, relax, and enjoy a peaceful home.


If you’re looking for a laid-back companion who loves quiet company, soft snuggles, and the occasional lazy day, this sweet boy might be your perfect match.

name: Kano
description_html:
Meet social butterfly Kano (with a butterfly marking on his head to prove it!): your future couch potato, walking buddy, and part-time tap dancer 💃🐶


At 7 years young Kano is a young senior bulldog (aka experienced, distinguished, and past his “eat your shoes” phase). He’s fully house trained, crate trained, dog friendly, people obsessed, and basically just out here trying to love everyone he meets.


His signature move? The morning tippy-tappys—a full performance the second he gets out of bed that will absolutely make your day every day.


Kano is a gentleman on leash, a big fan of car rides (windows down, ears flapping, living his best life), and always down for a good walk… followed immediately by a nap, obviously. He’s a social eater, so he prefers a little company at mealtime—think dinner date energy, not lonely microwave meal.


He’s been around cats a few times and is very curious but respectful—no drama, just vibes.


Sweet, affectionate, and full of quiet charm, Kano is the kind of dog who doesn’t need chaos to shine—he just needs a home ready for a whole lot of love.


Adopt Kano: because your couch deserves him. 🛋️


You won't find Kano at the shelter but you can reach out to his foster via adopets or email his advocates with questions at caring@classiccanines.org to inquire about Kano and set up a meeting with this love!

name: Tiger
description_html: Tiger is such a loving boy who walks very well on the leash and takes treats gently. He may want to eventually sit down in your lap and get some cuddles.

name: Gatanga
description_html: Gatanga came in with several siblings when their owner could no longer care for them. He is shy at first, but loves head pets and gets comfort from it. He also enjoys cuddling up with his cat friends, so he may enjoy a home with feline frienship!

name: Bunny
description_html: Bunny came in with her litter mates when their owner could no longer afford to keep them. She's a sensitive soul and prefers to hide when things are stressful or loud. Bunny would do best in a low traffic, quiet home and may do well with cat friends. It may take a little time to earn her trust, but once she settles in we think she will make a very loyal and affectionate companion!

name: Ginger
description_html:
Ginger recently arrived in our yard from parts unknown. She's a sweet babe, and she loves humans. We have a 7-year-old human, a 2-year-old family dog, and a 15-year-old cat. Ginger is enamored of the human, playful with the family dog, and curious about the cat from a safe distance.


Ginger's a young pup, so she's all potential. She needs exercise and attention to keep up with her appetite and social development. She's very quickly becoming crate trained, and she's excellent on a leash for her young age (no pulling and no leash aggression so far). Despite being chummy with the family dog, she's shown some food aggression and territorial behavior when family members show affection to the family dog. In our case, the family dog concedes without much of a tussle, but keep in mind that Ginger doesn't yet feel secure about having resources. She needs time and consistent, positive reinforcement.


If you're looking for a loyal, energetic, intelligent friend who brings joyful energy to your world, Ginger might just be the dog for you!

name: Daisy
description_html:
Daisy is a super sweet. She does well with other dogs and loves to spend her day relaxing while every once in a while playing with the occasional toy. She was found with her (presumed) sister, Rosie, and we would prefer they be adopted together as they are quite attached.


She is house and crate trained. She can be a little anxious on a leash at first, but once she trusts you she will follow you anywhere. Overall just a great, sweet dog. Please give her a chance to show you!

name: Rosie
description_html:
Rosie is a very sweet girl. She’s great with other dogs, calm mannered and a couch potato! She was found with her (presumed) sister, Daisy, and we would prefer they be adopted together as they are quite attached.


She is house and crate trained. She can be a bit anxious on a leash at first but once she trusts you it’s easy for her to just walk next to you. Overall just a great, sweet dog. Please give her a chance to show you!

name: Beaux
description_html:
Meet Beaux,


He's a Pit/Lab mix, with the temperament of a lab and the energy of a pit. He's the perfect family dog, as he's so calm, gentle, potty trained, and loves to cuddle. He is the most calm dog you will ever meet (we've never heard him bark); and for only being 1-year to 1-1/2 years old, he's got so much life ahead of him. He loves going on walks (will always stay with you) and sniffing everything. He's very chill around other dogs as well. He can be very independent and doesn't need to be cared for all day long. He's the perfect addition to any family and we have no doubt that you will fall in love with him immediately. He's got such a kind soul and a soft presence.

name: Kitty
description_html:
Kitty is the kind of dog who turns heads the moment you meet her. Her tall, perky ears stand straight up like little exclamation points, always ready to catch the sound of a friendly voice or the rustle of something interesting. She has a gentle, sweet demeanor that makes you feel instantly at ease, and you can tell there’s so much personality just waiting to blossom once she finds her person.

She carries a large scar along her back, a quiet reminder that her start in life wasn’t easy. Kitty can’t tell us what she’s been through, but what’s remarkable is how she refuses to let her past dim her view of people. She greets the world with hope, softness, and a willingness to trust again.

On walks, Kitty begins with enthusiasm, then settles into a slower pace where she can take in the world—sniffing the breeze, watching the scenery, and enjoying the simple pleasure of being outside with someone she likes. Her favorite spot on campus is the Real Life Room, where she melts into snuggles and shows us exactly what she’s dreaming of: a home of her own.

Kitty is a world-class cuddler. She loves chin scratches, gives the sweetest hugs, and has mastered the art of climbing onto a chair just to be closer to you for more affection. She’s tender, observant, and endlessly loving.

Kitty is ready for someone who sees her not for the scar on her back, but for the bright, beautiful future she’s eager to share. If you’re looking for a loyal companion with a gentle soul and a heart full of love, Kitty is waiting to meet you.

name: Luna Love
description_html:
Meet Luna Love, a smart lady with proven home experience - what can she do for you?
